name: BBQ Pork Lo Mein
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Bok choy, cut into 1/2-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook noodles in plenty of boiling water until al dente, then drain and toss with sesame oil; set aside.
2. Combine sauce ingredients; set aside.
3. Heat wok, add oil, and add ginger. Stir fry until fragrant (about 10 seconds), then add bok choy, bean sprouts, and pork. Stir fry for 2 minutes.
4. Add broth, then when the broth is hot, add noodles. Cover and cook for 2 minutes.
5. Add sauce, stir well, and serve.
Variations:
Instead of pork, use leftover cooked roast beef, lamb, shrimp, or chicken. Other fresh vegetables in season can be used in place of the sprouts and bok choy.
